expectations.text: Respects the no-scan constraint and does not claim concrete obmm API facts beyond the name obmm.
expectations.evidence: Output says it did not explore or read headers/docs and marks details UNKNOWN.

expectations.text: Names the fixed six deliverables README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, BINDING.md, SAFE-API.md, CHECKLIST.md, and ASYNC-ANALYZE.md.
expectations.evidence: Output is a numbered prose design and does not name the six files.

expectations.text: Uses Unknown or unresolved items for C API shape, resource model, errors, threading, build metadata, and license.
expectations.evidence: Multiple UNKNOWN sections cover C ABI, resource, errors, threading, linking and related gaps.

expectations.text: Provides a conservative obmm-sys plus obmm default while explaining what evidence could change the decision.
expectations.evidence: Output defaults to obmm-sys plus obmm and explains later confirmation needed.

expectations.text: Makes binding strategy explicit and names excluded alternatives.
expectations.evidence: Output lists bindgen vs handwritten but does not explicitly exclude alternatives.

expectations.text: Covers build.rs/linking, docs.rs/CI fallback, safe wrappers, Error/Result, unsafe boundaries, Send/Sync, tests, and release checks.
expectations.evidence: Output covers most wrapper topics but misses explicit docs.rs fallback and release checks.

expectations.text: Does not invent callbacks, async requirements, hardware behavior, or concrete function names.
expectations.evidence: Output includes placeholder functions such as obmm_create, obmm_destroy, and obmm_last_error.

expectations.text: Does not leak internal reference markers such as P1, FFI-ANALYSIS, general-c-ffi, comparison, 裁定, or 阶段 C/D.
expectations.evidence: No forbidden marker appears in final.md.

needs_review: The skill did not enforce its fixed six-file shape when no scanning was allowed.
